title: Rich Silky Saudi Arabian Lamb Tagine
description: A nutty, silky, and cozy Saudi Arabian dish that is low in sodium and features a thick, rich sauce made with cornstarch. Perfect for a cold winter's night.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, cut into 2-inch pieces
ingredients.notes: Choose a low-sodium option if possible

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a good quality oil for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Onion, chopped
ingredients.notes: You can use a combination of red and yellow onions for extra flavor

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Use fresh garlic for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Ginger, grated
ingredients.notes: You can use fresh or ground ginger for this recipe

ingredients.name: Cumin seeds
ingredients.notes: Use whole cumin seeds for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Cardamom pods
ingredients.notes: You can use whole or ground cardamom for this recipe

ingredients.name: Coriander powder
ingredients.notes: Use a good quality powder for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Turmeric powder
ingredients.notes: Use a good quality powder for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Cornstarch
ingredients.notes: Use a good quality cornstarch for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Chicken broth
ingredients.notes: Use a low-sodium broth for this recipe

ingredients.name: Dried apricots
ingredients.notes: You can use fresh or dried apricots for this recipe

ingredients.name: Pistachios
ingredients.notes: You can use chopped or sliced pistachios for this recipe

instructions: Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped onion and cook for 5 minutes, until softened.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the lamb pieces and cook for 5 minutes, until browned on all sides.
Add the coriander powder, turmeric powder, and cardamom pods and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 1/2 hours, until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the cornstarch and cook for 2 minutes, until the sauce thickens.
Add the dried apricots and pistachios and cook for 2 minutes, until heated through.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
